w:r.w:t: CareXpert is a stateless, session-scoped web application. No user accounts are required and no documents are persisted beyond a single browser session. The architecture follows a thin-client / thick-server pattern: the browser handles upload, display, and interactivity while a lightweight API server owns all document processing and AI orchestration.
File upload, results rendering, side-by-side toggle, checklist interactivity
Static asset delivery, SSL termination, global low-latency routing
Request validation, file pre-processing, AI orchestration, response shaping
Multimodal document parsing, structured JSON extraction, plain-language generation
Text layer extraction from PDF files before AI processing
Temporary result caching per request ID; TTL 5 minutes; no DB
1. User uploads document → browser sends multipart/form-data POST to /api/analyse
2. API server validates file type, size, and MIME signature
3. File is routed to the correct parser: pdf-parse (PDF) | Tesseract (image) | pass-through (text)
4. Extracted raw text + optional metadata (age, language) are assembled into a structured AI prompt
5. Prompt is sent to Claude API with a strict system prompt that prohibits external knowledge
6. Claude responds with a JSON object containing all 5 output sections
7. API server validates JSON schema, shapes response, and returns it to the client
8. Client renders each section; no data is written to any database
V1 targets a single-region deployment sufficient for a hackathon demo and early production. Scale-out path is documented in Section 8.
Local only; mock AI responses available
React 18 with Vite for build tooling. No heavy UI framework — Tailwind CSS utility classes for styling. Keeps the bundle small and the setup fast for a hackathon timeline.
Drag-and-drop zone + file picker; accepts PDF, JPG, PNG, TXT; client-side size gate (10 MB)
Age input (numeric, 1–120); Language toggle (EN / HI); both sent as query params
Disabled until valid file selected; shows spinner on click; prevents double-submit
Animated step labels: Uploading → Extracting → Simplifying → Done (estimated ~8s)
Renders 5 sections; tab navigation; Compare toggle
Plain-language diagnosis in a readable card layout
Responsive HTML table: Medicine | Dosage | Timing | Duration
Alert-style cards per side effect; red CTA for doctor trigger
Interactive tick-list; checked state in local React state only (not persisted)
Single sentence in large type; Copy to Clipboard button
Side-by-side split view: original extracted text (left) | simplified output (right)
Non-blocking banner for parse warnings; blocking modal for fatal errors
Zero use of localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies for document content.
All interactive elements keyboard-navigable; ARIA labels on file input and results.
Responsive breakpoints for 360px–1440px. Primary use case is a smartphone browser.
React error boundaries around ResultsPage; fallback UI with retry option.
Browser never calls Claude API directly. All AI traffic routes through the backend.
Matches Vercel/Railway defaults; async I/O suits file streaming
Minimal, well-understood; no overhead from full frameworks
Multipart upload parsing; memory storage (no disk write)
No external OCR API dependency; runs in-process
Official typed client; handles retries and streaming
Validate AI JSON response before returning to client
Fast unit + integration tests; no Jest config overhead
Reject if MIME type not in [application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png, text/plain].
Reject if file size > 10 MB (enforced at Multer config level, not just client).
Return 400 with a structured error body on any validation failure.
Raw text string; all pages concatenated
OCR text string; confidence score attached
The system prompt is a fixed, version-controlled string loaded from an env-referenced file. It explicitly instructs the model to:
Extract only what is written in the document. Add nothing.
Return a single valid JSON object matching the OutputSchema (see Section 5).
Use the patient’s age (if provided) to adjust reading level, not medical content.
Respond entirely in the specified language (default: English).
If a field cannot be found in the document, set its value to null — never infer.

After receiving the Claude response, the backend runs Zod schema validation on the parsed JSON. If validation fails, the server returns a 502 with a structured error rather than passing corrupt data to the client. This is the medication safety gate.
Validated JSON is returned as-is to the client wrapped in a standard response envelope. No transformation beyond null-to-empty-string coercion for display safety.
CRITICAL: The system prompt is the primary patient safety control.
• Do not suggest alternative medicines or dosages.
• Do not add information not present in the uploaded document.
• Do not interpret ambiguous instructions — surface them as-is.
• Medication table values must be copied verbatim from the document.
• If OCR confidence is flagged low, prepend a [LOW CONFIDENCE] tag to the field.
The system prompt must be reviewed and approved before any production deployment.
V1 has no persistent database. All processing is stateless and session-scoped.
The schema below is the proposed structure for V2 when user history and audit logging are added.
It is included here to ensure V1 design decisions do not conflict with future data needs.
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
input_type VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, -- pdf | image | text
ocr_used BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
session_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
event VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- upload_received | ocr_complete | ai_complete | error
occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
Raw uploaded files are never written to disk or object storage. Only extracted text is stored (optional, V2).
medications, side_effects, and follow_up_items stored as JSONB for flexible querying without rigid normalization overhead.
V1 and proposed V2 schema contain no user ID, email, or device fingerprint. Privacy by design.
Kept in a separate table so it can be independently purged or exported for compliance without touching result data.
All responses use Content-Type: application/json. All errors follow RFC 7807 Problem Details format.
Upload document and receive structured output
Liveness check; returns service status and version
Readiness check; verifies AI API connectivity
file File Required. PDF, JPG, PNG, or TXT. Max 10 MB.
"plain_text": "Your doctor found that you have Type 2 Diabetes...",
"family_summary": "Patient has Type 2 Diabetes; taking Metformin twice daily; follow-up blood test in 30 days."
Uploaded file is not PDF, image, or text
AI determined document is not a prescription or discharge summary
Text extraction produced empty or unusable output
AI response did not match expected JSON schema after 2 retries
Too many requests from same IP (see Section 8)
Claude API returned an error or timed out
Unexpected server error; safe error message returned

CareXpert V1 is a stateless, anonymous tool. No login, no accounts, no session tokens. This is intentional and aligned with the PRD non-goals. The privacy guarantee is simpler without auth: if there is no identity, there is no PII linkage.
The Claude API key must never be exposed to the browser.
It is stored as a server-side environment variable (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
The Node.js backend is the sole caller of the Anthropic API.
Any architecture that calls the AI API directly from the browser is a security failure.
30 seconds per request; kills hung AI calls
Origin whitelist: production domain only in prod
When persistent history is added in V2, the recommended approach is:
No password. User enters email, receives a one-time login link. Reduces friction for a non-technical patient user base.
Short-lived access tokens (15 min) + refresh tokens (7 days). Stored in httpOnly cookies.
Google/Facebook login adds complexity and privacy surface without meaningful benefit for this use case.
HIGH — core AI dependency; pin version
Removes a major dependency surface. If V2 adds PostgreSQL, use Drizzle ORM (lightweight, type-safe).
No cloud OCR (Google Vision / AWS Textract):
Avoids sending patient documents to a third party. Tesseract.js runs in-process.
Use exact version in package.json (e.g., 0.20.1, not ^0.20.1). A silent model swap or API change could break the medication table schema.
Language output is handled by the Claude prompt. No Google Translate dependency.
Return 502 with retry-after header; display user-friendly 'Service busy' message
No fallback needed for static assets at V1 scale
Acceptable for V1; upgrade to Pro for production SLA
V1 is designed for demonstrability, not massive scale. The constraints below define the acceptable operating envelope before architectural changes are needed.
Single Node.js process; Tesseract.js is CPU-bound
Within Railway Starter + Anthropic free tier limits
OCR on complex images can reach 8–9s alone
Depends on Claude API load and prompt length
Horizontal scaling via Railway replicas or AWS ECS; stateless design enables this without code changes
Move OCR to a dedicated worker queue (BullMQ + Redis); decouple from HTTP request cycle
Cache results by document content hash (SHA-256); identical uploads skip AI call
PostgreSQL on Railway or Neon (serverless Postgres); schema defined in Section 4
Stream to S3-compatible storage (Cloudflare R2); process from URL, not buffer
Move to Redis-backed rate limiter (rate-limit-redis) so limits survive restarts
8.4 What NOT to Optimise in V1
Do not build a job queue for V1. A synchronous HTTP request-response cycle is simpler, easier to debug, and sufficient for hackathon scale.
Do not add Redis, message brokers, or worker pools to V1. These add operational complexity with no benefit below ~100 concurrent users.
Do not cache AI responses in V1. The risk of serving a cached (potentially wrong) medication table outweighs the cost saving.
Anthropic API key. Never committed to source control. Injected via CI secrets.
Pinned Claude model string, e.g. claude-sonnet-4-20250514
Override default 10 MB limit. Useful for testing.
Rate limit window in ms. Default 3600000 (1 hour).
Max requests per window per IP. Default 20.
Max request duration in ms. Default 30000.
Path to the system prompt file. Loaded at server start.
Allowed origin in production. Must match deployed frontend URL.
Flag low-confidence results with [LOW CONFIDENCE] in output
V2: integrate Google Vision API with explicit consent
Pin SDK version; Zod validation catches schema breaks
Monitor Anthropic changelog; version-lock model string
Single point of failure (one Railway instance)
Acceptable for hackathon; Railway auto-restarts on crash
Per PRD: no data retained. Acceptable until regulated.
Pre-warm on server boot, not on first request
CareXpert Technical Requirements Document • V1.0 • March 2026
